sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) lightning. (Syriac, Arab. ܒܰܪܩܳܐ, بَرْقُ id.) Dan 10:6. Coll. lightnings, Psa 144:6 2Sa 22:15 Eze 1:13. Pl. בְּרָקִים Job 38:35 Psa 18:15 77:19 , etc. Applied to the brightness of a sword, Eze 21:15, 33 Eze 21:33. Deu 32:41, בְּרַק חַרְבִּי “the lightning of my sword,” i.e. my glittering sword. Nah 3:3 Hab 3:11. Comp. Zec 9:14. Hence-

(2) poet. the glittering sword itself, Job 20:25.

(3) [Barak], pr.n. of a leader of the Israelites, who by the aid of Deborah obtained a great victory over the Canaanites, Jud. 4:6 , seq.; 5:1 , 12 Jdg 5:12, 15 Jdg 5:15. Comp. בְּדָן. He was called “thunderbolt,” fulmen (Cic. pro Balb. xv.), as amongst the Pœni “Hamilcar Barcas.”
